channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lithuania vows to boost defense spending to 5-6% of GDP because of threat of Russian aggression
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Lithuania has decided to raise its spending on defense to between 5% and 6% of overall national economic output starting in 2026 due to the threat of Russian aggression in the region, Lithuanian President Gitanas Nausėda said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungarian minister sanctioned by US 'strengthened' by the penalty, Prime Minister Orbán says
item.description: Hungary's prime minister says sanctions issued by the United States last week against a powerful Hungarian official have “strengthened” that official’s standing within the populist government.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel's security cabinet convenes to approve deal to release hostages and pause the 15-month war
item.description: Israel’s security cabinet convened to decide whether to approve a deal that would release dozens of hostages held by militants in Gaza and pause the 15-month-war.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 lawyers for the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny are jailed by a Russian court
item.description: Three lawyers who once represented the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny have been jailed in Russia as part of the Kremlin’s crackdown on dissent that has reached levels unseen since Soviet times.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: French president in Lebanon to meet the country's new leaders and discuss ceasefire with Israel
item.description: France’s president began a visit to Lebanon Friday, where he will meet the crisis-hit country’s newly elected leaders, as the nation attempts to recover from the 14-month Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pakistani court sentences ex-PM Imran Khan and his wife to 14 and 7 years in prison in graft case
item.description: A defense lawyer says a Pakistani court has sentenced the country’s already-imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan and his wife to 14 and seven years in jail after finding them guilty of corruption.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia and Iran have a troubled history despite their current alliance
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin is hosting his Iranian counterpart Friday for the signing of a broad pact between Moscow and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says deal to release hostages held in Gaza has been reached after last minute snags
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Friday a deal to return hostages held in the Gaza Strip has been reached, after his office said earlier there were last minute snags in finalizing a ceasefire that would pause 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Xi's special representative Han Zheng to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: China’s vice president will attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration, according to a Thursday statement from the Foreign Ministry Spokesperson. “At U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: He is credited with one of history's most indelible photos. A new documentary questions who took it
item.description: It is one of the 20th century's most memorable images: a naked girl, screaming, running from a napalm bombing during the Vietnam War.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Bank of England governor Mark Carney enters race to be Canada’s next prime minister
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Mark Carney, the first non-Brit to run the Bank of England since it was founded in 1694 and the former head of Canada’s central bank, said Thursday he is entering the race to be Canada’s next prime minister following the resignation of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu throws curveball into Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is tapping the brakes on claims of success by President Biden, President-elect Trump and partners in the Middle East that a ceasefire and hostage release deal has been achieved to pause 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US foes Iran, Russia, other UN diplomats exempt from NYC congestion pricing as taxpayers forced to foot bill
item.description: The U.S. State Department has exempted U.N. diplomats and missions in New York City from paying new congestion fees according to recent guidance published by the government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of survivors and dead pulled from abandoned South African mine as hundreds remain underground
item.description: Using a cage-like device, South African rescuers have brought at least 60 bodies and 92 survivors to the surface from one of the country's deepest mines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India steps up diplomatic relations with the Taliban as rival Pakistan loses influence in Afghanistan
item.description: India and Afghanistan have taken a significant step in securing their first high-level bilateral engagement. No foreign government, including India, officially recognizes the Taliban regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VIDEO: 84-year-old man fights off robber using pair of jeans
item.description: YORKSHIRE, U.K. (WFLA) — An 84-year-old man used a pair of jeans to fight off a would-be robber as he did his laundry in South Yorkshire.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Polish town invites Musk to buy castle for his European HQ
item.description: Tech billionaire is reportedly looking for a sizeable location, possibly a castle in Italy, as a hub for his operations in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tsunami alert lifted after magnitude 6.6 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan issued a tsunami advisory on Monday after a magnitude 6.6 earthquake struck the southwest of the country. The public warnings to stay away from coastal areas were later lifted. The agency initially gave the magnitude estimate at 6.9, but later revised it down.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, NewsNation
